originalTitle: Asia-Pacific markets open lower as Iran-U.S. negotiations remain in focus
summary: Asia-Pacific markets opened lower on Thursday as investors parse the ongoing Iran-U.S. negotiations
content:
Asia-Pacific markets opened lower on Thursday, as investors continue to assess mixed signals from the ongoing Iran-U.S. negotiations amid a fragile ceasefire.

Secretary of State Marco Rubio said that talks with Iran have made some progress and that "we're going to give it every chance to succeed," adding that the U.S. prefers "the negotiated diplomatic route." That said, President Donald Trump has said that he will not permit Iran to control the key Strait of Hormuz as part of a deal.

While a Reuters report said that Tehran had committed to restoring commercial traffic through Hormuz to prewar levels within one month of an agreement with the U.S., citing Iranian state media, the White House said in a social media post that the report about a memorandum of understanding was "a complete fabrication."

West Texas Intermediate futures for July were 1.86% higher at $90.33 per barrel as of 8:04 p.m. ET. Brent crude futures for July rose 1.87% to $96.05 per barrel.

South Korea's Kospi was lower at 0.29% while the small-cap Kosdaq slid 0.25%. Japan's Nikkei 225 fell 0.76%, while the Topix declined 0.71%. Australia's S&P/ASX 200 was 0.75% lower.

S&P 500 futures and Nasdaq 100 futures both rose less than 0.1%. Futures tied to the Dow Jones Industrial Average added 49 points, or 0.1%.

During Wednesday's regular session, S&P 500 ticked 0.02% higher to 7,520.36, another closing record. The 30-stock Dow gained 182.60 points, or 0.36%, for a record close of 50,644.28. The Nasdaq Composite edged up 0.07% to end at 26,674.73.

— CNBC's Lisa Kailai Han contributed to this report.
chineseTitle: 由于伊朗和美国关系紧张，亚太市场开盘走低谈判仍是焦点
chineseSummary: 周四亚太市场开盘走低，因投资者分析正在进行的伊朗-美国争端。谈判
chineseContent:
亚太市场周四开盘走低，投资者继续评估伊朗与美国之间持续紧张局势发出的复杂信号。在脆弱的停火状态下进行谈判。

美国国务卿马可·卢比奥表示，与伊朗的谈判已取得一些进展，“我们将给予其成功的一切机会”，并补充说，美国更喜欢“通过谈判达成的外交路线”。尽管如此，唐纳德·特朗普总统表示，作为协议的一部分，他不会允许伊朗控制关键的霍尔木兹海峡。

虽然路透社援引伊朗官方媒体的报道称，德黑兰承诺在与美国达成协议后一个月内将通过霍尔木兹的商业交通恢复到战前水平，但白宫在社交媒体帖子中表示，有关谅解备忘录的报道“完全是捏造的”。

截至晚上 8:04，西德克萨斯中质原油 7 月期货上涨 1.86%，至每桶 90.33 美元。等。 7 月份布伦特原油期货上涨 1.87%，至每桶 96.05 美元。

韩国综合指数下跌 0.29%，小型股科斯达克指数下跌 0.25%。日本日经225指数下跌0.76%，东证指数下跌0.71%。澳大利亚 S&P/ASX 200 指数下跌 0.75%。

标准普尔 500 指数期货和纳斯达克 100 指数期货均上涨不到 0.1%。与道琼斯工业平均指数相关的期货上涨 49 点，即 0.1%。

在周三的常规交易中，标准普尔 500 指数上涨 0.02%，至 7,520.36 点，再创收盘纪录。道指 30 种股票上涨 182.60 点，涨幅 0.36%，收于创纪录的 50,644.28 点。纳斯达克综合指数微升 0.07%，收于 26,674.73 点。

— CNBC 的 Lisa Kailai Han 对本报告作出了贡献。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
